explanations.7: This creates a special folder mount that saves your bash command history between container restarts, so you don't lose your terminal history.
explanations.8: This shares Docker's control socket with the container so you can run Docker commands from inside the container itself.
explanations.9: This uses a named volume to store node_modules separately so npm/yarn packages persist and don't get lost when the container restarts.
explanations.10: This stores VS Code extensions in a separate volume so you don't have to reinstall them every time you start the container.
explanations.15: When port 3000 is forwarded, VS Code will automatically open it in your browser instead of just showing a notification.
explanations.30: This references environment variables to dynamically set the container user ID and group ID, which prevents file permission problems.
explanations.36: When you save a markdown file, VS Code will automatically run all available fixes from the linter to clean up the formatting.
explanations.70: This tells the spell-checker to connect to a LanguageTool service running on a different container at that network address.
explanations.82: This customizes the VS Code window title to show the project name and remind you that you're working inside a development container.
